Declares a function for calculating the remainder of two ECMA numbers.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares a function for performing arithmetic operations on numbers within the ECMA (European Computer Manufacturers Association) framework. The file defines a function prototype [`ecma_op_number_remainder`](<#ecma_op_number_remainder>), which calculates the remainder of the division between two `ecma_number_t` type numbers. The header file includes a guard to prevent multiple inclusions and includes the `ecma-globals.h` file, which likely contains global definitions and types used in the ECMA framework. This file is part of a larger library or application that deals with number arithmetic operations in compliance with ECMA standards.

# Function Declarations (Public API)

### ecma\_op\_number\_remainder<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_op_number_remainder}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/operations/ecma-number-arithmetic.h#L28>)

Calculates the remainder of two numbers.
- **Description**: Use this function to find the remainder when dividing two numbers. It handles special cases such as when either operand is NaN, when the left operand is infinity, or when the right operand is zero, by returning NaN. If the right operand is infinity or the left operand is zero while the right is not, it returns the left operand. This function is useful in arithmetic operations where remainder calculation is needed, and it must be called with valid number inputs.
- **Inputs**:
    - `left_num`: The dividend in the remainder operation. It can be any number, including NaN or infinity. If it is NaN or infinity, the function returns NaN.
    - `right_num`: The divisor in the remainder operation. It can be any number, including NaN or infinity. If it is NaN or zero, the function returns NaN. If it is infinity, the function returns the left operand.
- **Output**: Returns the remainder of the division of `left_num` by `right_num`, or NaN in special cases as described.
- **See Also**: [`ecma_op_number_remainder`](<ecma-number-arithmetic.c.md#ecma_op_number_remainder>)  (Implementation)
